question.prompt: When was the hepatotoxicity signal first detectable in the data?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: The signal was first detectable around Week 8, when the high-dose arm mean ALT began diverging from other arms. However, individual values were still within normal range, and the DSMB Review 2 concluded "liver function within normal limits." The 4-6 week lag from dose initiation to ALT elevation meant the earliest monitoring windows showed clean data.
key_facts: the hepatotoxicity signal has a 4-6 week lag from dose initiation making early monitoring reviews appear clean
retrospective trajectory analysis revealed the safety signal was present from Week 8 but missed in real-time monitoring
ALT elevations in the XR-7491 trial are dose-dependent with the 30mg arm showing the clearest trajectory

The XR-7491 clinical trial program encompasses Rheumatoid Arthritis (RA), Cardiovascular (CV), and Oncology (ONC-204) studies, spanning episodes ep_001 through ep_012 and associated biomarker trials. The program timeline extends from 2023 screening phases to 2025 interim analyses and monitoring snapshots.

**Rheumatoid Arthritis (XR-7491) Progression:**
Enrollment has expanded across 12 sites (SITE-001 to SITE-012) with randomized subjects increasing from initial cohorts to 98 total by ep_012.
*   **Site-007 (PI_Garcia):** On 2025-03-21, 65 subjects were enrolled (Subjects_014/015). Interim analysis reported 52% ACR20 response (P=0.04) and 1.5 DAS28 reduction (P=0.01) [ep_007].
*   **Site-008 (PI_Miller):** By 2025-03-24, 68/100 subjects were randomized. Report EFF-005 indicated dose-dependent efficacy: Placebo 40%, 30mg 65%, Combined 54%, with a 1.6 DAS28 reduction [ep_008].
*   **Site-009 (PI_Taylor):** A 2025-03-27 snapshot showed 75/78 randomized (1:1:1:1). Protocol v2.0 (2025-02-15) adjusted dosing. Subject_018 (Placebo) recorded AST 45 U/L. 16 minor AEs occurred, 2 requiring intervention [ep_009].
*   **Site-010 (PI_Anderson):** Product accountability on 2025-03-30 confirmed a zero balance for Study XR-7491-RA-002. Form FDA-1572 (ID 1572-2025-001) scored 100. Drug XR-7491-L-004 (Expiry 2026-03-30) was stored at 20C-25C [ep_010].
*   **Site-011 (PI_Thomas):** On 2025-04-02, 90/120 subjects were enrolled. Interim ACR20 was 60% (Placebo 48%, 30mg 72%) with 1.9 DAS28 reduction. Subject_022 showed AST 50 U/L. 20 mild AEs (AE-017, AE-020) were ongoing [ep_011].
*   **Site-012 (PI_Jackson):** By 2025-04-05, 98 total subjects were enrolled, 95 randomized (SUBJECT_024 on 30mg, SUBJECT_025 on 5mg). 22 minor AEs occurred. The DSMB flagged ALT elevations in the 30mg arm. Moderate RASH (AE-003) was noted in SUBJECT_003 [ep_012].
*   **Site-013 (PI_White):** On 2025-04-08, 105 enrolled, 102 randomized. Subject_026 (AST 42 U/L) and Subject_002 (AST 41 U/L) showed elevated liver enzymes. 24 minor AEs recorded [clinical_trial_10_ep_013].
*   **Site-014 (PI_Harris):** On 2025-04-11, 115 enrolled total. Subject_028 and Subject_029 enrolled in ARM-03 (30mg). 26 minor AEs included Injection Site Reaction (Subject_028) and Back Pain (Subject_029) [clinical_trial_10_ep_014].
*   **Site-015 (PI_Martin):** On 2025-04-14, 120 enrolled, 118 randomized. Subject_030 (F, 54) and Subject_031 (M, 59) received 30mg but were randomized to ARM-03 (protocol defined as 15mg) [clinical_trial_10_ep_015].
*   **Site-016 (PI_Thompson):** On 2025-04-17, 130 enrolled, 125 randomized. Subject_032 (F, 55) and Subject_033 (M, 60) enrolled. AE-003 (Fatigue) and AE-004 (Injection Site Reaction) occurred. DSMB Review 13 noted ALT elevations in the 30mg arm, recommending enhanced monitoring [clinical_trial_10_ep_016].

**Cardiovascular (CV) Progression:**
*   **Site-007 (Dr. G. Black):** Enrollment reached 142/500. AE-007 (Fracture, Grade 1, SUB-007, 2023-11-01) was recorded. Total AEs: 45 [dx_cardiology_trial_007].

**Oncology (ONC-204) Progression:**
*   **Memorial Oncology Center (MOC-001, Dr. A. Vance):** 118 randomized, 2 withdrew. AE-204-001 (Rash Papular, Grade 2, S-1042, 2023-04-12, Dose Hold) and AE-204-002 (Fatigue, Grade 1, S-1055) occurred [dx_oncology_biomarker_001].
*   **Regional Cancer Institute (RCI-001, Dr. K. Wu):** 92 randomized by 2023-05-20. S-1088 (Grade 3 Hypothyroidism, 2023-05-02, Dose Reduction) and S-1091 (Grade 1 Pruritus). PK: S-1088 Cmax 138.2 ng/mL, S-1091 Cmax 142.5 ng/mL [dx_oncology_biomarker_002].
*   **University Medical Center (US-001, Dr. S. Patel):** 102 randomized by 2023-06-02. S-1102 (Grade 2 Psoriasis, 2023-05-10, Dose Interruption). Missed dose DL-204-001. PK Cmax 142.0 ng/mL [dx_oncology_biomarker_003].
*   **Screening & Toxicity:** Screening dropped from 60 (June 2023) to 5 (Dec 2023), a 72.5% screen failure rate. AE-204-009 (Diarrhea, Grade 2, S-1160, 2023-07-01, Dose Delay) and AE-204-010 (Colitis, Grade 3, S-1172, 2023-07-04, Discontinuation) were recorded. ctDNA: S-1160 75 copies/mL, S-1172 50 copies/mL. Dose: 200 mg IV Q3W [clinical_trial_10_dx_oncology_biomarker_004/005].
*   **St. Jude Research Hospital (PI Dr. M. Jones):** 390 screened, 98 enrolled, 96 randomized (2023-07-25). Subjects S-1190, S-1201 received 200 mg IV [clinical_trial_10_dx_oncology_biomarker_006].
*   **National Cancer Center (PI Dr. T. Brown):** 420 screened, 115 enrolled, 112 randomized (2023-08-12). Subjects S-1220, S-1235. AE-204-013 (Headache, Grade 1) and AE-204-014 (Erythema, Grade", "metadata": {}}
